name: speedtest-cli

metadata.summary: Command line interface for testing internet bandwidth using speedtest.net

documentation:
# speedtest-cli

Command line interface for testing internet bandwidth using
speedtest.net

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/speedtest-cli.svg
:alt: Latest Version
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/speedtest-cli/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/travis/sivel/speedtest-cli.svg
:alt: Travis
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/speedtest-cli/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/speedtest-cli.svg
:alt: License
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/speedtest-cli/
```

## Versions

speedtest-cli works with Python 2.4-3.7

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/speedtest-cli.svg
:alt: Versions
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/speedtest-cli/
```

## Installation

### pip / easy_install

```
pip install speedtest-cli
```

or

```
easy_install speedtest-cli
```

### Github

```
pip install git+https://github.com/sivel/speedtest-cli.git
```

or

```
git clone https://github.com/sivel/speedtest-cli.git
cd speedtest-cli
python setup.py install
```

### Just download (Like the way it used to be)

```
wget -O speedtest-cli https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sivel/speedtest-cli/master/speedtest.py
chmod +x speedtest-cli
```

or

```
curl -Lo speedtest-cli https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sivel/speedtest-cli/master/speedtest.py
chmod +x speedtest-cli
```

## Usage

```
$ speedtest-cli -h
usage: speedtest-cli [-h] [--no-download] [--no-upload] [--single] [--bytes]
                     [--share] [--simple] [--csv]
                     [--csv-delimiter CSV_DELIMITER] [--csv-header] [--json]
                     [--list] [--server SERVER] [--exclude EXCLUDE]
                     [--mini MINI] [--source SOURCE] [--timeout TIMEOUT]
                     [--secure] [--no-pre-allocate] [--version]

Command line interface for testing internet bandwidth using speedtest.net.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
https://github.com/sivel/speedtest-cli

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --no-download         Do not perform download test
  --no-upload           Do not perform upload test
  --single              Only use a single connection instead of multiple. This
                        simulates a typical file transfer.
  --bytes               Display values in bytes instead of bits. Does not
                        affect the image generated by --share, nor output from
                        --json or --csv
  --share               Generate and provide a URL to the speedtest.net share
                        results image, not displayed with --csv
  --simple              Suppress verbose output, only show basic information
  --csv                 Suppress verbose output, only show basic information
                        in CSV format. Speeds listed in bit/s and not affected
                        by --bytes
  --csv-delimiter CSV_DELIMITER
                        Single character delimiter to use in CSV output.
                        Default ","
  --csv-header          Print CSV headers
  --json                Suppress verbose output, only show basic information
                        in JSON format. Speeds listed in bit/s and not
                        affected by --bytes
  --list                Display a list of speedtest.net servers sorted by
                        distance
  --server SERVER       Specify a server ID to test against. Can be supplied
                        multiple times
  --exclude EXCLUDE     Exclude a server from selection. Can be supplied
                        multiple times
  --mini MINI           URL of the Speedtest Mini server
  --source SOURCE       Source IP address to bind to
  --timeout TIMEOUT     HTTP timeout in seconds. Default 10
  --secure              Use HTTPS instead of HTTP when communicating with
                        speedtest.net operated servers
  --no-pre-allocate     Do not pre allocate upload data. Pre allocation is
                        enabled by default to improve upload performance. To
                        support systems with insufficient memory, use this
                        option to avoid a MemoryError
  --version             Show the version number and exit
```

## Python API

See the [wiki](https://github.com/sivel/speedtest-cli/wiki).

## Inconsistency

It is not a goal of this application to be a reliable latency reporting tool.

Latency reported by this tool should not be relied on as a value indicative of ICMP
style latency. It is a relative value used for determining the lowest latency server
for performing the actual speed test against.

There is the potential for this tool to report results inconsistent with Speedtest.net.
There are several concepts to be aware of that factor into the potential inconsistency:

1. Speedtest.net has migrated to using pure socket tests instead of HTTP based tests
2. This application is written in Python
3. Different versions of Python will execute certain parts of the code faster than others
4. CPU and Memory capacity and speed will play a large part in inconsistency between
   Speedtest.net and even other machines on the same network

Issues relating to inconsistencies will be closed as wontfix and without
additional reason or context.

functions.name: speedtest.to_utf8
documentation: No-op encode to utf-8 for py3

functions.name: speedtest.print_
documentation: Wrapper function for py3 to print, with a utf-8 encoded stdout

functions.name: speedtest.to_utf8
documentation: Encode value to utf-8 if possible for py2

functions.name: speedtest.print_
documentation:
The new-style print function for Python 2.4 and 2.5.

Taken from https://pypi.python.org/pypi/six/

Modified to set encoding to UTF-8 always, and to flush after write

functions.name: speedtest.create_connection
documentation:
Connect to *address* and return the socket object.

Convenience function. Connect to *address* (a 2-tuple `(host,
port)`) and return the socket object. Passing the optional
*timeout* parameter will set the timeout on the socket instance
before attempting to connect. If no *timeout* is supplied, the
global default timeout setting returned by {func}`getdefaulttimeout`
is used. If *source_address* is set it must be a tuple of (host, port)
for the socket to bind as a source address before making the connection.
An host of '' or port 0 tells the OS to use the default.

Largely vendored from Python 2.7, modified to work with Python 2.4

functions.name: speedtest._build_connection
documentation:
Cross Python 2.4 - Python 3 callable to build an `HTTPConnection` or
`HTTPSConnection` with the args we need

Called from `http(s)_open` methods of `SpeedtestHTTPHandler` or
`SpeedtestHTTPSHandler`

functions.name: speedtest.build_opener
documentation:
Function similar to `urllib2.build_opener` that will build
an `OpenerDirector` with the explicit handlers we want,
`source_address` for binding, `timeout` and our custom
`User-Agent`

functions.name: speedtest.get_exception
documentation:
Helper function to work with py2.4-py3 for getting the current
exception in a try/except block

functions.name: speedtest.distance
documentation: Determine distance between 2 sets of [lat,lon] in km

functions.name: speedtest.build_user_agent
documentation: Build a Mozilla/5.0 compatible User-Agent string

functions.name: speedtest.build_request
documentation:
Build a urllib2 request object

This function automatically adds a User-Agent header to all requests

functions.name: speedtest.catch_request
documentation:
Helper function to catch common exceptions encountered when
establishing a connection with a HTTP/HTTPS request

functions.name: speedtest.get_response_stream
documentation:
Helper function to return either a Gzip reader if
`Content-Encoding` is `gzip` otherwise the response itself

functions.name: speedtest.get_attributes_by_tag_name
documentation:
Retrieve an attribute from an XML document and return it in a
consistent format

Only used with xml.dom.minidom, which is likely only to be used
with python versions older than 2.5

functions.name: speedtest.print_dots
documentation:
Built in callback function used by Thread classes for printing
status

functions.name: speedtest.ctrl_c
documentation:
Catch Ctrl-C key sequence and set a SHUTDOWN_EVENT for our threaded
operations

functions.name: speedtest.version
documentation: Print the version

functions.name: speedtest.csv_header
documentation: Print the CSV Headers

functions.name: speedtest.parse_args
documentation: Function to handle building and parsing of command line arguments

functions.name: speedtest.validate_optional_args
documentation:
Check if an argument was provided that depends on a module that may
not be part of the Python standard library.

If such an argument is supplied, and the module does not exist, exit
with an error stating which module is missing.

functions.name: speedtest.printer
documentation: Helper function print a string with various features

functions.name: speedtest.shell
documentation: Run the full speedtest.net test

classes.name: speedtest.FakeShutdownEvent
documentation:
Class to fake a threading.Event.isSet so that users of this module
are not required to register their own threading.Event()

classes.name: speedtest._Py3Utf8Output
documentation:
UTF-8 encoded wrapper around stdout for py3, to override
ASCII stdout

Server provided as a speedtest mini server does not actually appear

Unable to successfully POST results to speedtest.net API after

classes.name: speedtest.SpeedtestUploadTimeout

testlength configuration reached during upload
Used to ensure the upload halts when no additional data should be sent

classes.name: speedtest.SpeedtestBestServerFailure
documentation: Unable to determine best server

classes.name: speedtest.SpeedtestMissingBestServer
documentation: get_best_server not called or not able to determine best server

classes.name: speedtest.SpeedtestHTTPConnection

methods.name: speedtest.SpeedtestHTTPConnection.connect
documentation: Connect to the host and port specified in __init__.

Custom HTTPConnection to support source_address across
Python 2.4 - Python 3

classes.name: speedtest.SpeedtestHTTPSConnection
documentation:
Custom HTTPSConnection to support source_address across
Python 2.4 - Python 3

classes.name: speedtest.SpeedtestHTTPHandler
documentation:
Custom `HTTPHandler` that can build a `HTTPConnection` with the
args we need for `source_address` and `timeout`

classes.name: speedtest.SpeedtestHTTPSHandler
documentation:
Custom `HTTPSHandler` that can build a `HTTPSConnection` with the
args we need for `source_address` and `timeout`

classes.name: speedtest.GzipDecodedResponse
documentation:
A file-like object to decode a response encoded with the gzip
method, as described in RFC 1952.

Largely copied from `xmlrpclib`/`xmlrpc.client` and modified
to work for py2.4-py3

classes.name: speedtest.HTTPDownloader
documentation: Thread class for retrieving a URL

classes.name: speedtest.HTTPUploaderData
documentation:
File like object to improve cutting off the upload once the timeout
has been reached

classes.name: speedtest.HTTPUploader
documentation: Thread class for putting a URL

classes.name: speedtest.SpeedtestResults

methods.name: speedtest.SpeedtestResults.share
documentation:
POST data to the speedtest.net API to obtain a share results
link

methods.name: speedtest.SpeedtestResults.dict
documentation: Return dictionary of result data

methods.name: speedtest.SpeedtestResults.csv_header
documentation: Return CSV Headers

methods.name: speedtest.SpeedtestResults.csv
documentation: Return data in CSV format

methods.name: speedtest.SpeedtestResults.json
documentation: Return data in JSON format

Class for holding the results of a speedtest, including:

Download speed
Upload speed
Ping/Latency to test server
Data about server that the test was run against

Additionally this class can return a result data as a dictionary or CSV,
as well as submit a POST of the result data to the speedtest.net API
to get a share results image link.

classes.name: speedtest.Speedtest

methods.name: speedtest.Speedtest.get_config
documentation:
Download the speedtest.net configuration and return only the data
we are interested in

methods.name: speedtest.Speedtest.get_servers
documentation:
Retrieve a the list of speedtest.net servers, optionally filtered
to servers matching those specified in the `servers` argument

methods.name: speedtest.Speedtest.set_mini_server
documentation:
Instead of querying for a list of servers, set a link to a
speedtest mini server

methods.name: speedtest.Speedtest.get_closest_servers
documentation:
Limit servers to the closest speedtest.net servers based on
geographic distance

methods.name: speedtest.Speedtest.get_best_server
documentation:
Perform a speedtest.net "ping" to determine which speedtest.net
server has the lowest latency

methods.name: speedtest.Speedtest.download
documentation:
Test download speed against speedtest.net

A `threads` value of `None` will fall back to those dictated
by the speedtest.net configuration

methods.name: speedtest.Speedtest.upload
documentation:
Test upload speed against speedtest.net

A `threads` value of `None` will fall back to those dictated
by the speedtest.net configuration
Class for performing standard speedtest.net testing operations
